$description: DefenseClaw environment-variable registry. Single source of truth for every DEFENSECLAW_* env var consumed by the codebase. Both internal/envvars (Go) and cli/defenseclaw/envvars (Python) load this file. The CI gate at cli/tests/test_envvars_codebase_coverage.py fails the build if any callsite references a DEFENSECLAW_* var not declared here. Auto-generated docs (docs/ENV-VARS.md, docs-site/content/docs/reference/env-vars.mdx) are rendered from this file.

$categories.security_opt_out: Toggles that weaken security defaults. Surfaced by 'defenseclaw doctor' whenever active.
$categories.telemetry: OTel / observability config. Override values normally set in config.yaml.
$categories.runtime_path: Path / binary overrides for installation and runtime layout.
$categories.hook_internal: Set by hook scripts and the daemon launcher. Do not override manually.
$categories.upgrade_internal: Set only by trusted upgrade controllers during fresh-process and staged bridge handoffs. Do not override manually.
$categories.credential: Auth materials (API keys, tokens, passwords). Should reference env vars, not contain raw values in code.
$categories.splunk_bridge: Splunk-bridge bundle (bundles/splunk_local_bridge). Not consumed by the Go gateway.
$categories.test_fixture: Test-only fixtures. Should never be set in production.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PREPAIR_TRUST_DEVICE_KEY
entries.purpose: Bypass the provenance-sentinel check during 'defenseclaw sandbox setup' pre-pairing. When the gateway has not yet created the .provenance sibling next to device.key, set this to 1 to fall back to the legacy loose acceptance of any 32-byte blob found at data_dir/device.key.

consumers.description: _pre_pair_device gates the provenance fail-closed branch on this var

entries.security_note: Provenance-sentinel verification — prevents acceptance of an unauthenticated device.key file (e.g. a copy left on disk by a prior install or attacker).
entries.replacement_hint: Restart the gateway once after upgrading; LoadOrCreateIdentity auto-writes the .provenance sentinel and the env var is no longer needed.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CODEX_LOOPBACK_TRUST
entries.purpose: Restore legacy loopback-trusts-any-bearer behavior for the Codex connector. With this unset, codex requests on 127.0.0.1 must present an Authorization that matches a recorded provider key. Set to 1 on shared hosts where multiple users share the same OS account.

consumers.description: Authenticate() falls back to legacy behavior when set; emits a [SECURITY] log line

entries.security_note: Per-bearer Codex authentication on loopback — prevents same-host user-to-user impersonation when multiple users share an OS account.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED
entries.purpose: Skip checksum / signature verification during 'defenseclaw upgrade' or scripts/upgrade.sh. Intended for emergency-fix scenarios where the signing CA chain is briefly unavailable.

consumers.description: Python upgrade path checks this before downloading without a checksum
Shell upgrade path checks this before proceeding without verification

entries.security_note: Upgrade-artifact integrity — prevents installing a tampered tarball/wheel pulled from a hijacked release CDN or MITM.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OPENSHELL_ALLOW_UNPINNED
entries.purpose: Accept a mutable OCI tag (e.g. 'latest') when installing openshell-sandbox instead of requiring a content-addressed digest or sha256 pin. Recommended only for local development.

consumers.description: Skips integrity verification when set

entries.security_note: Pinned-digest sandbox install — prevents tag-mutation supply-chain attacks where an upstream tag is silently re-pointed at a malicious image.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OPENSHELL_ARCH_DIGEST
entries.purpose: Pin the openshell-sandbox install to a specific platform manifest digest (sha256:...). Recommended pinning method for production. Read in conjunction with DEFENSECLAW_OPENSHELL_ALLOW_UNPINNED.

consumers.description: Verifies OCI manifest digest against this pin

entries.security_note: Pinned-digest sandbox install — content-addressed verification of the OCI manifest before extraction.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OPENSHELL_BINARY_SHA256
entries.purpose: Pin the final extracted openshell-sandbox binary to a specific sha256. Alternative to DEFENSECLAW_OPENSHELL_ARCH_DIGEST for cases where only the binary checksum is published.

consumers.description: Marker check (presence enables sha256 verification path)
Verifies extracted binary sha256 against this pin

entries.security_note: Pinned-digest sandbox install — sha256 verification of the extracted binary.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_TARBALL_SHA256
entries.purpose: Operator-provided sha256 pin for the gateway tarball downloaded by `defenseclaw upgrade`. Verified before extraction. When neither a sidecar .sha256 file nor this pin is supplied, the upgrade aborts unless DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED=1.

consumers.description: Python upgrade verifier
Shell upgrade verifier

entries.security_note: Operator-supplied checksum pin for upgrade artifacts — defense-in-depth alongside the sidecar .sha256 file.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_WHL_SHA256
entries.purpose: Operator-provided sha256 pin for the Python CLI wheel downloaded by `defenseclaw upgrade`. Verified before installation. When neither a sidecar .sha256 file nor this pin is supplied, the upgrade aborts unless DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED=1.

consumers.description: Python upgrade verifier
Shell upgrade verifier

entries.security_note: Operator-supplied checksum pin for upgrade artifacts — defense-in-depth alongside the sidecar .sha256 file.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_FRESH_PROCESS
entries.purpose: Marks the isolated installed-CLI child used for an upgrade handoff so the child cannot recursively hand off again and a newly installed gateway can delegate its readiness wait to that supervising controller.

consumers.description: Rejects recursive fresh-process upgrade handoffs and marks the isolated child process
Delegates gateway readiness to the supervising upgrade controller while retaining safe launch and immediate-exit checks
Scopes the marker to the immutable post-hard-cut controller continuation

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PHASE_TWO_MUTATOR_CHILD
entries.purpose: Marks the trusted phase-two mutation child so upgrade recovery can distinguish the lease-holding subprocess from its controller.

consumers.description: Marks the private mutation subprocess while it inherits the authenticated upgrade lease

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OBSERVABILITY_V8_PREFLIGHT_BINDING
entries.purpose: Carries the controller-authenticated observability-v8 preflight snapshot into the fresh target migration child. It binds the validated config and recovery plan to the phase-two mutation process.
accepted_values: JSON object no larger than 4096 bytes

consumers.description: Passes the verified binding only to the isolated phase-two migration child
Requires and validates the binding before the v7-to-v8 hard-cut mutation

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_MUTATION_TOKEN
entries.purpose: Carries the authenticated 32-character lowercase-hex upgrade attempt identifier into migration writers so attempt-owned temporary files can be named and cleaned without touching unrelated files.
entries.default: unset (writers use their ordinary temporary-file names)

consumers.description: Passes the phase-one recovery plan identifier to the isolated migration process
Tags atomic config-write temporary files as owned by the current upgrade attempt
Tags atomic migration-write temporary files as owned by the current upgrade attempt
Tags atomic migration-state temporary files as owned by the current upgrade attempt

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_TEST_MODE
entries.purpose: Explicitly enables the loopback-only unpublished release endpoint used by upgrade protocol release-candidate tests.

consumers.description: Gates the fresh-controller candidate release endpoint override

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_BASH
entries.purpose: Selects the Bash executable used by release-workflow tests so macOS CI can exercise wrappers with the system Bash runtime.
entries.default: unset (auto-detect Bash, including Git Bash on Windows)
accepted_values: absolute path to a Bash executable

consumers.description: Selects the Bash executable used to run POSIX release-workflow contract tests

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_PHASE1_RECOVERY_CRASH
entries.purpose: Injects a process-kill cut point while replaying the POSIX phase-one gateway publication journal so crash recovery is tested at each ownership boundary.

consumers.description: Kills the isolated journal-recovery helper at the selected publication cut point
Drives deterministic crash-and-replay coverage for both recovery cut points

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_PHASE1_ROLLBACK_CRASH
entries.purpose: Injects a process-kill cut point after POSIX phase-one state restoration but before controller artifact restoration so durable mixed-progress recovery is tested.

consumers.description: Kills the same-session rollback controller after exact source state is durable
Proves the next fresh recovery process accepts source root metadata and completes artifact restoration

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_PHASE1_ACTIVE_SEAL_CRASH
entries.purpose: Injects a process kill after the authenticated POSIX phase-one active manifest is durable but before its digest is committed to the outer recovery journal.

consumers.description: Kills the seal helper and parent controller at the orphan-manifest recovery boundary
Proves an authenticated orphan active manifest remains usable for fresh-process rollback

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_PHASE1_MIGRATION_CRASH
entries.purpose: Injects a process kill after the POSIX bridge migration has changed canonical config but before an active-state manifest can be sealed.

consumers.description: Kills the fixture migration between canonical mutations
The bridge journal arms state-mutation recovery before fixture-controlled migration execution

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_PHASE1_POST_HEALTH_CRASH
entries.purpose: Injects a process kill after the POSIX bridge gateway passes health but before phase-one journal closure.

consumers.description: Kills the bridge controller at the live-gateway post-health recovery boundary
Proves fresh recovery stops the live bridge using the bound split DataDir and config path

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UPGRADE_TEST_RELEASE_BASE_URL
entries.purpose: Carries a numeric-loopback HTTP release asset base URL across the fresh-controller handoff for unpublished candidate testing.

consumers.description: Overrides the GitHub release download base only when upgrade test mode is explicitly enabled

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_STAGED_UPGRADE
entries.purpose: Marks a trusted resolver-to-controller staged hard-cut handoff before rollback artifacts are accepted.

consumers.description: Requires the staged handoff marker before consuming resolver-supplied rollback artifacts
Unix release resolver sets the marker for the fresh installed controller
Windows release resolver sets the marker for the fresh installed controller

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_STAGED_BRIDGE_VERSION
entries.purpose: Carries the authenticated bridge version across a staged hard-cut handoff; the fresh controller requires it to equal the installed source version.

consumers.description: Matches staged rollback artifacts to the installed bridge version
Unix release resolver supplies the verified bridge version
Windows release resolver supplies the verified bridge version

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_STAGED_BRIDGE_ARTIFACT_DIR
entries.purpose: Carries the private directory of authenticated bridge rollback artifacts into the fresh hard-cut controller.
accepted_values: absolute owner-only directory (0700 on POSIX; protected owner-only DACL on Windows)

consumers.description: Revalidates and retains resolver-supplied rollback artifacts before installed state changes
Unix release resolver supplies the retained artifact directory
Windows release resolver supplies the retained artifact directory

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_STAGED_TARGET_CONTROLLER_VERSION
entries.purpose: Binds a staged hard-cut handoff to the authenticated target-controller version that the resolver installed out of place.

consumers.description: Requires the fresh target controller to match the resolver-authenticated target version before accepting bridge rollback custody
Unix release resolver supplies the authenticated target version to the fresh out-of-place controller

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DUMP_RAW_SECRETS
entries.purpose: E2E test toggle ONLY. When set, scripts/test-e2e-full-stack.sh prints raw secrets to its diagnostic logs. Never set in production.

consumers.description: Dumps raw secrets in diagnostic output for test debugging

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DISABLE_REDACTION
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input. When truthy during defenseclaw upgrade, the converter preserves the legacy unredacted posture with explicit v8 profile none. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Reads the captured v7 upgrade environment and materializes the equivalent v8 redaction profile

entries.replacement_hint: Configure observability.defaults, bucket, or destination redaction_profile in config v8.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_REVEAL_PII
entries.purpose: Reveal PII in operator-facing logs only (CLI stdout, TUI). Persistent sinks (audit DB, JSONL, OTel) still redact. Weaker than DISABLE_REDACTION.

consumers.description: Reveal() reads this env var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_WEBHOOK_ALLOW_LOCALHOST
entries.purpose: Relax the webhook SSRF guard to permit RFC1918 / loopback / link-local destinations. Required for local-development setups (e.g. webhook against http://127.0.0.1:5000); a production setup should not use this.

consumers.description: Webhook sender SSRF gate
Webhook validate-on-add SSRF gate
Python writer validate-on-add

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_JSONL_DISABLE
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input. When truthy during defenseclaw upgrade, the converter omits the legacy implicit JSONL destination. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Reads the captured v7 upgrade environment when converting implicit JSONL behavior

entries.replacement_hint: Add, disable, or remove an explicit kind: jsonl destination in config v8.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DISABLE_AWS_HTTP1_SHIM
entries.purpose: Disable the AWS Bedrock HTTP/1 monkey-patch the OpenClaw plugin installs to make Bedrock traffic visible to the guardrail proxy. Disabling means Bedrock traffic bypasses the proxy.

consumers.description: JS shim bails out when set

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UNGUARDED_CHATGPT_CODEX_RESPONSES
entries.purpose: Allow ChatGPT Codex response-backend requests to bypass the guardrail proxy. Intended only as an explicit availability fallback when the Codex backend cannot operate through the proxy.

consumers.description: Env-var name exported for the OpenClaw interceptor and tests
isCodexResponsesPassthroughEnabled gates the explicit unguarded passthrough path
Interceptor warning names the active unguarded bypass

entries.security_note: Codex response guardrail enforcement — prevents model prompt/response traffic from silently bypassing observe/action policies unless the operator explicitly opts into an unguarded fallback.
entries.replacement_hint: Leave unset for normal guarded operation; use only while diagnosing proxy incompatibility.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_FORCE_AWS_HTTP1_SHIM
entries.purpose: Force the AWS HTTP/1 shim to install even on non-Bedrock setups. Counterpart to DEFENSECLAW_DISABLE_AWS_HTTP1_SHIM; only one should be set at a time.

consumers.description: JS shim forces install when set

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TOOL_INSPECT_FAIL_OPEN
entries.purpose: Make the plugin-side tool-inspect hook fail-open (allow tool) when the gateway is unreachable. Default is fail-closed (block).

consumers.description: OpenClaw plugin tool-inspect handler

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_POLICY_VALIDATE_ALLOW_NO_OPA
entries.purpose: Accept a policy file as 'validated' even when OPA / Rego is not installed. Default rejects; this turns validation into a no-op when OPA is absent.

consumers.description: Policy validate command bypass

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DEV
entries.purpose: Mark the process as a developer build. Disables credential-placeholder redaction so test fixtures can use literal example keys.

consumers.description: isCredentialScrubDevMode reads this var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST
entries.purpose: Mark the process as running under tests. Same effect as DEFENSECLAW_DEV on credential-placeholder redaction.

consumers.description: isCredentialScrubDevMode reads this var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_FAIL_MODE
entries.purpose: Per-shell override of guardrail.hook_fail_mode for malformed, unauthorized, or incomplete gateway responses. Transport failures are controlled separately by DEFENSECLAW_STRICT_AVAILABILITY. Templated into every *-hook.sh.

consumers.description: Representative shared template; every generated hook reads the same override

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_STRICT_AVAILABILITY
entries.purpose: Opt-IN to fail-closed on transport errors during hook execution. Inverse of the bypass pattern. Not a bypass; stricter than default.

consumers.description: Hook hardening sourced by every *-hook.sh

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DEBUG
entries.purpose: Gateway client logs every request/response frame to stderr. Verbose; do not enable in production.

consumers.description: Client struct gates verbose logging on this var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_WEBHOOK_DEBUG
entries.purpose: Webhook dispatcher dumps full request bodies (including secrets) to stderr.

consumers.description: Webhook sender debug field

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_LLM_DEBUG
entries.purpose: Python LLM bridge logs per-request prompt + response bodies. May contain secrets.

consumers.description: LLM bridge _DEBUG flag

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_JUDGE_TRACE
entries.purpose: LLM judge logs every prompt + response. May contain agent traffic and secrets.

consumers.description: Judge debug toggle

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PERSIST_JUDGE
entries.purpose: Persist every judge prompt + response to disk under data_dir. Disk grows fast; use only during analysis.

consumers.description: Gateway boot enables judge persistence

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SIDECAR_DIAG
entries.purpose: Extra sidecar boot-time diagnostics (config dump, env presence). Off by default to keep operator logs tight.

consumers.description: sidecarDiagEnabled helper

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_ENABLED
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former otel.enabled master toggle. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Preserves the effective v7 OTel master-toggle decision during upgrade
Binds the variable only inside the legacy configuration loader used before v8 activation

entries.replacement_hint: Enable or disable named observability.destinations entries in config v8.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_ENDPOINT
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former flat OTLP exporter endpoint. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts the captured v7 endpoint into a canonical v8 destination
Reads the endpoint only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_PROTOCOL
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former flat OTLP exporter protocol. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts the captured v7 protocol into a canonical v8 destination
Reads the protocol only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_TLS_INSECURE
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former flat OTLP TLS-insecure toggle. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.
entries.security_note: Enabling this variable disables OTLP server-certificate verification after legacy configuration is migrated into a named destination.

consumers.description: Converts the captured v7 TLS policy into a canonical v8 destination
Reads the TLS decision only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_TRACES_ENDPOINT
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former trace-specific OTLP endpoint. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific endpoints into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific endpoints only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure a v8 destination endpoint or signal_overrides.traces.endpoint.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_TRACES_PROTOCOL
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former trace-specific OTLP protocol. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific protocols into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific protocols only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure the protocol on the canonical v8 destination.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_LOGS_ENDPOINT
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former log-specific OTLP endpoint. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific endpoints into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific endpoints only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure a v8 destination endpoint or signal_overrides.logs.endpoint.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_LOGS_PROTOCOL
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former log-specific OTLP protocol. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific protocols into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific protocols only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure the protocol on the canonical v8 destination.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_METRICS_ENDPOINT
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former metric-specific OTLP endpoint. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific endpoints into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific endpoints only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure a v8 destination endpoint or signal_overrides.metrics.endpoint.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_METRICS_PROTOCOL
entries.purpose: Upgrade-only v7 migration input for the former metric-specific OTLP protocol. The v8 runtime ignores this variable.

consumers.description: Converts captured v7 signal-specific protocols into canonical v8 destinations
Reads signal-specific protocols only inside the legacy configuration loader

entries.replacement_hint: Configure the protocol on the canonical v8 destination.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
entries.purpose: Local-observability-stack-only toggle. Used by bundles/local_observability_stack/docker-compose.yml to gate telemetry collection. Distinct from DEFENSECLAW_OTEL_ENABLED.

consumers.description: Compose-file env reference

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_RUN_ID
entries.purpose: Correlation ID stamped on every event for cross-sink joins. Auto-generated per gateway boot if unset; explicit value is useful for E2E test runs.

consumers.description: Go reader
Audit store reader
Sidecar boot
Python logger reader
Python DB reader
E2E test runner default

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOME
entries.purpose: Override the canonical data dir (default ~/.defenseclaw). Honored by every Python and Go entry point.

consumers.description: Go default-resolver
Python config loader
Connector path resolver
Installer reads this
Upgrader reads this
Hooks read this

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CONFIG
entries.purpose: Override the config.yaml path independently from DEFENSECLAW_HOME. Used by managed-enterprise services to keep administrator-owned config under /etc/defenseclaw while runtime state remains under /var/lib/defenseclaw.

consumers.description: Go config path resolver
Python config path resolver
Migration config path resolver

entries.security_note: Managed config path separation — lets system services read admin-owned policy without making the runtime data directory admin-writable.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DEPLOYMENT_MODE
entries.purpose: Pin the process deployment mode independently from config.yaml. Managed service definitions set this to managed_enterprise so a service-writable config replacement cannot downgrade enterprise trust enforcement.

consumers.description: Pins deployment mode before parsing or trusting config.yaml
Managed Linux service pin
Managed macOS service pin

entries.security_note: Immutable managed-mode boundary — prevents config replacement from disabling administrator-owned path and ownership checks.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PLIST_SRC
entries.purpose: Override the LaunchDaemon plist source file the macOS installer copies to /Library/LaunchDaemons. Precedence: --plist flag > this env var > plist next to install.sh > repo-tree plist. Intended for admin bootstrap scripts that ship a pre-modified plist alongside the DefenseClaw bundle.

consumers.description: macOS bundle installer plist resolution

entries.security_note: Installer-only override — treated as untrusted operator input, so the referenced plist must be root-owned and never group/world-writable. The bundled default plist next to install.sh is accepted regardless of extraction owner (content originates from the trusted bundle) but group/world-writable is still refused.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_GUARDIAN_AUTH_DIR
entries.purpose: Select the administrator-owned directory containing the hook guardian protected-target authorization ledger.

consumers.description: Resolves the protected-target authorization path
Privileged guardian writes successful protected targets
Managed health verifies connector coverage before advertising enforcement

entries.security_note: Privileged repair authorization — must remain root/admin-owned and non-writable by the DefenseClaw service account.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_IPC_SOCKET
entries.purpose: Override the local UDS gRPC socket path used by the internal/ipc server in unmanaged and test deployments. Managed-enterprise resolution deliberately ignores this environment variable; use administrator-owned managed.socket_path instead.
entries.default: unset (resolver picks per-mode default: <dirname(data_dir)>/ipc/defenseclaw_ipc.sock for managed_enterprise, otherwise <data_dir>/ipc/defenseclaw_ipc.sock)

consumers.description: ResolveSocketPath consults this before falling back to the deployment-mode default

entries.security_note: Local-only socket path override — managed_enterprise ignores the environment override, creates the resolved socket with a 0660 ceiling (0600 otherwise), and authenticates peers against the effective code-signing team, signing, and bundle-ID allowlists.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_GUARDIAN_CONNECTOR
entries.purpose: Systemd template override naming the connector reconciled by an enterprise hook guardian instance.

consumers.description: Documented systemd template override for per-user hook guardian instances

entries.security_note: Template-scoped connector selection — avoids broad home scanning by keeping each privileged guardian instance bound to an explicit connector target.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_GUARDIAN_AGENT_VERSION
entries.purpose: Systemd template override supplying the local agent version used for enterprise hook-contract validation.
entries.default: unset (falls back to cached discovery version when available)

consumers.description: Documented systemd template override for action-mode hook contract validation

entries.security_note: Action-mode hook contract validation — lets managed deployments pin the agent version the guardian validates before writing blocking hooks.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_GATEWAY_BIN
entries.purpose: Override path to the defenseclaw-gateway binary. Used by CI / packagers when the binary isn't on PATH.

consumers.description: Python gateway-process spawner

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_BIN
entries.purpose: Override path to the defenseclaw CLI binary. Used by tests and setup-llm.sh.

consumers.description: Plugin test harness
LLM setup script

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OBSERVABILITY_BIN
entries.purpose: Override the executable used by the backward-compatible POSIX local-observability bridge.

consumers.description: POSIX compatibility bridge selects the lifecycle-controller executable

entries.security_note: Executable selection — a non-default value changes which local program the compatibility bridge launches.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_OVERLAY_ROOT
entries.purpose: Extra provider-catalog overlay dir merged on top of the built-in catalog.

consumers.description: Provider setup overlay loader

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CUSTOM_PROVIDERS_PATH
entries.purpose: Path to a custom providers YAML file consulted before the embedded catalog.

consumers.description: Go embedded-providers loader

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_API_ADDR
entries.purpose: Sidecar API address that hooks dial. Templated into every *-hook.sh and shim. Typically set by the gateway setup; operator overrides only for unusual layouts.
entries.default: (templated value from gateway.api_port at hook install time)

consumers.description: Hooks dial this

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SIDECAR_URL
entries.purpose: Target URL for the bundled CodeGuard skill (skills/codeguard/main.py) to call into the sidecar.

consumers.description: Skill sidecar URL

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DIR
entries.purpose: Used in generated openshell-sandbox shell wrappers to locate the DefenseClaw install dir from inside the sandbox.

consumers.description: Sandbox shell wrappers reference this

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_VENV
entries.purpose: Path to the DefenseClaw uv venv. Set by install.sh / upgrade.sh; operators normally don't override.

consumers.description: Installer venv path
Upgrader venv path

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_INSTALL_DIR
entries.purpose: Directory where CLI symlinks are placed by install.sh / setup-llm.sh.

consumers.description: Install location for setup-llm

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_INSTALL_ROOT
entries.purpose: Native Windows installer root injected by the stable defenseclaw.exe launcher so the managed Python CLI can locate the owning packaged install for installer-based upgrade handoff.

consumers.description: Launcher appends the verified install root to the managed Python process environment
Windows native-upgrade tests cover rejection of an untrusted injected install root

entries.security_note: Installer root selection — upgrade code must treat this inherited value as untrusted and verify it is a canonical, product-owned Windows install before handing off to the setup executable.

entries.name: MIGRATION_DEFENSECLAW_HOME
entries.purpose: Passed by scripts/upgrade.sh to the migration step. Identifies the data dir the migration should operate on.

consumers.description: Upgrade-time migration runner

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_CONNECTOR
entries.purpose: Internal label identifying which connector's hook is executing. Set by every *-hook.sh; do not override manually.

consumers.description: Each hook exports this

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CONNECTOR
entries.purpose: Runtime selector for the connector that owns an invocation of the shared hook scripts. Managed installations read the installer-owned connector sidecar; non-managed shells may set this explicitly.

consumers.description: Selects one unambiguous connector runtime record for shared hooks
Writes and validates the connector-scoped runtime sidecar

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_NAME
entries.purpose: Internal label identifying which hook is executing. Set by every *-hook.sh.

consumers.description: Each hook exports this

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_HOME
entries.purpose: Hardened HOME exported by hooks to insulate them from operator HOME.

consumers.description: Hook hardening

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_CWD
entries.purpose: Resolved CWD exported by hooks; used by sanitizeHookCWD to bound git operations.

consumers.description: Hook hardening

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_PATH
entries.purpose: Hardened PATH exported by hooks (system-only) so a hostile workspace can't shadow git/curl/etc.

consumers.description: Hook hardening

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_MAX_BODY
entries.purpose: Request-body cap (in bytes) for hooks. Default 1048576 (1 MiB).

consumers.description: Body-cap enforcement

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_MANAGED_HOOK
entries.purpose: Immutable sentinel baked into managed-enterprise hook scripts so missing authentication material and unavailable enforcement fail closed even when an inherited environment requests permissive availability behavior.
entries.default: unset (set to 1 only by generated managed hooks)

consumers.description: Managed hook templates set and export the sentinel from trusted generated script content
Missing-token and transport-failure handling forces fail-closed behavior when the sentinel is set

entries.security_note: Managed enforcement fail-closed boundary — the value is generated by the privileged hook installer and is not an operator security opt-out.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DAEMON
entries.purpose: Sentinel set by the daemon launcher in the child process so it knows it's the daemon.

Sidecar references in setup messages

entries.purpose: Short-lived secret transport for 'defenseclaw setup observability add'. The macOS app sets this in the child process environment so provider tokens are not exposed in command-line arguments; the CLI persists the value under the preset-specific token environment variable.

consumers.description: Click reads the setup token from the child process environment instead of argv

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_WEBHOOK_SECRET
entries.purpose: Generic webhook HMAC secret default.

entries.purpose: SIEM webhook secret default.

entries.purpose: Default registry auth env var (e.g. SkillsHub). Operators override per registry via cmd_registry.

consumers.description: Registry default auth_env

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SKILLSSH_TOKEN
entries.purpose: Example registry-specific token env var. Documented in cmd_registry.

consumers.description: Registry example token

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TRUSTED_BIN_PREFIXES
entries.purpose: Extra trusted binary prefixes for AI Discovery's binary probing, separated by os.pathsep (':' on POSIX, ';' on Windows). The default trust list covers system bin dirs, Homebrew (incl. Caskroom), MacPorts, and global node_modules trees; operators with non-standard layouts (e.g. /opt/mycorp/bin) can trust additional roots. Prefer managing via 'defenseclaw setup trusted-paths add|remove|list', which validates the directory and persists this variable to ~/.defenseclaw/.env; interactive action-mode setup offers the same remediation inline.

consumers.description: Agent discovery binary probe
setup trusted-paths CLI and inline trust prompt persistence

entries.security_note: Tight binary-discovery trust list — prevents PATH-shadow elevation where a malicious binary in a user-writable dir gets probed and treated as a real agent runtime. 'trusted-paths add' refuses world-writable and non-absolute directories unless --force.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_ANTHROPIC_PROBE_MODEL
entries.purpose: Override the model used by 'defenseclaw doctor' to probe Anthropic API key validity. Default is claude-3-5-haiku-latest; override for accounts with restricted model lists.

consumers.description: Doctor's Anthropic probe

entries.purpose: Placeholder LLM key used in test fixtures only.

consumers.description: Test fixture

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_KEY_NOTSET_12345
entries.purpose: Placeholder env var name used to assert 'unset' behavior in tests.

consumers.description: Test fixture for unset assertions

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_LLM_KEY
entries.purpose: Placeholder LLM key used in some test setups when DEFENSECLAW_LLM_KEY needs an alternate target.

consumers.description: Test fixture

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_ALLOW_CGNAT
entries.purpose: Allow RFC 6598 carrier-grade NAT addresses (100.64.0.0/10, used by Tailscale and similar overlays) through the SSRF / private-IP guards. When unset, CGNAT targets are blocked like other reserved ranges.

consumers.description: cgnatAllowed() omits 100.64.0.0/10 from the reserved-CIDR list when set
_cgnat_allowed() gates webhook URL validation for CGNAT IPs
_cgnat_allowed() gates registry/manifest SSRF resolution for CGNAT

entries.security_note: CGNAT egress guard — prevents SSRF into shared carrier-grade NAT / overlay-network address space (e.g. another tenant's Tailscale node).

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_ALLOW_PRIVATE_UPSTREAMS
entries.purpose: Comma-separated list of specific IP addresses exempt from the SSRF private-address block for LLM upstream forwarding. Merges with guardrail.allow_private_upstreams in config.yaml. Loopback, link-local, and cloud metadata IPs are never exempted.

consumers.description: ParseAllowedPrivateUpstreams() reads this env var and merges with config.yaml entries
_allowed_private_ips() reads this env var for Python-side SSRF guard exemption

entries.security_note: Private-upstream SSRF guard exemption — allows operator-specified on-prem LLM gateways on RFC 1918 addresses to bypass the private-IP block. Each IP must be explicitly listed (no CIDR).

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_ALLOW_HOOK_CONTRACT_DRIFT
entries.purpose: Bypass the action-mode fail-closed checks for unverified hook contracts and hook-contract lock drift during connector setup, allowing enforcing hooks to be installed against agent versions outside the verified contract matrix.

consumers.description: Blocks connector setup on an unverified hook contract in action mode unless set
Blocks connector setup on hook-contract lock drift in action mode unless set
Python setup path mirrors the gateway drift/contract enforcement

entries.security_note: Hook contract verification — prevents installing an enforcing hook whose request/response contract has drifted from the verified matrix (a silent guardrail bypass).

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_ALLOW_LOCAL_MCP_TARGETS
entries.purpose: Skip SSRF validation for MCP scan target URLs, permitting loopback, private, link-local, and cloud-metadata destinations. Intended for local development only.

consumers.description: validateMCPScanTargetURL() returns early without SSRF checks when set

entries.security_note: MCP scan-target SSRF guard — prevents a scan request from reaching IMDS / loopback / private hosts.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_BAKED_HOOK_PATH
entries.purpose: Baked into the installed _hardening.sh helper at hook-generation time. When non-empty, replaces the hardened hook PATH with this colon-separated directory list instead of the default system paths.

consumers.description: defenseclaw_harden_env() uses it to set the locked-down hook PATH

entries.security_note: Hook PATH lockdown — a mis-baked value that widens PATH could re-admit attacker-writable directories to hook execution.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_DATA_DIR
entries.purpose: Marker injected by the daemon launcher into the spawned gateway child process environment, recording which data directory that child belongs to. Not an operator-tunable knob; the data directory is configured via config.yaml.
entries.default: unset (set by the daemon on child processes)

consumers.description: Written into the gateway child env to tag the owning data directory

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_GATEWAY_ADDR
entries.purpose: Gateway API address for the Windows native Go hook entrypoint. Resolution precedence: explicit flag, then this env var, then the hooks/.hookcfg sidecar, then the configured default. Non-loopback values are rejected and replaced with 127.0.0.1:<api_port>.

consumers.description: Primary env resolution of the hook's gateway API address
Sidecar .hookcfg fallback for the same key

entries.security_note: Loopback-only enforcement — a non-loopback gateway address is rejected so a hook cannot be redirected to an off-box collector.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_PATH_TRUSTED
entries.purpose: Companion name to DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_PATH that an agent process might set. The hook-hardening preamble explicitly unsets it before locking down PATH, so a compromised agent cannot mark a hostile PATH as trusted. There is no read site — the variable is intentionally stripped.

consumers.description: unset DEFENSECLAW_HOOK_PATH_TRUSTED during env hardening (anti-consumed)

entries.security_note: Hook PATH lockdown — stripped so an agent-supplied 'trusted' flag cannot re-enable an attacker-controlled PATH.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_JUDGE_PERSIST_QUEUE_SIZE
entries.purpose: Emergency boot-time override for the async judge-persistence queue depth. Controls how many judge bodies can buffer before drops are recorded (defenseclaw.judge.persist.drops, reason=queue_full). Normally set via guardrail.judge_persist_queue_depth in config.yaml.

consumers.description: Overrides cfg.Guardrail.JudgePersistQueueDepth at sidecar boot

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SANDBOX_FORCE_REGEX_CLEANUP
entries.purpose: Opt into the legacy broad regex cleanup of sandbox/openshell network namespaces when no saved namespace file exists. Used by the generated pre-sandbox and cleanup-sandbox scripts.

consumers.description: Embedded pre-sandbox script reads it before namespace cleanup
Embedded cleanup-sandbox script reads it

entries.security_note: Namespace cleanup scope — a broad regex sweep can delete unrelated network namespaces on a shared host.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TERRAFORM_PLUGIN_DIR
entries.purpose: Optional Terraform provider plugin directory for offline / cached provider installs when running 'defenseclaw setup splunk-o11y-dashboards'.

consumers.description: --plugin-dir Click option bound to this env var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_O11Y_DASHBOARD_NAME_PREFIX
entries.purpose: Prefix label applied to the Splunk Observability dashboard groups, dashboards, and detectors created by the Terraform setup command. Useful for smoke-test isolation.

consumers.description: --name-prefix Click option bound to this env var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SPLUNK_O11Y_DASHBOARDS_WORK_DIR
entries.purpose: Terraform working directory for Splunk Observability dashboard provisioning. Overrides the default under the DefenseClaw data directory.

consumers.description: --work-dir Click option bound to this env var

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TRACEPARENT
entries.purpose: W3C traceparent header value propagated from the agent/hook environment into outbound hook HTTP requests and the Codex telemetry bridge, enabling distributed-trace correlation.

consumers.description: Native hook reads it (precedence over TRACEPARENT / OTEL_TRACEPARENT)
Bash hook trace-context extraction
Codex telemetry bridge forwards it on outbound curls

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TRACESTATE
entries.purpose: W3C tracestate header value propagated alongside traceparent for vendor-specific trace baggage on hook and Codex telemetry outbound requests.

consumers.description: Native hook reads it alongside traceparent
Bash hook trace-context extraction
Codex telemetry bridge forwards it on outbound curls

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TRUSTED_PROXY_CIDRS
entries.purpose: Comma-separated CIDRs (or bare IPs) of reverse-proxy peers whose X-Forwarded-For header is trusted for client-IP attribution in logs. When unset, forwarded headers are ignored and the socket peer IP is authoritative.

consumers.description: isTrustedProxyPeer() trusts X-Forwarded-For only from these peers

entries.security_note: Client-IP attribution — trusting forwarded headers from an untrusted peer lets a caller spoof the source IP recorded in auth-failure and audit logs.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TUI_SKIP_FIRST_RUN_PROMPT
entries.purpose: Skip the interactive first-run setup wizard prompt when launching the TUI, proceeding directly without asking whether to run setup.

consumers.description: Gates the interactive first-run setup prompt

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CLEANUP_PARENT_PID

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CLEANUP_JOURNAL
entries.purpose: Protected native Setup transaction journal used to bind delayed directory cleanup to the uninstall transaction that scheduled it.

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CLEANUP_TRANSACTION_ID
entries.purpose: Exact native Setup uninstall transaction identifier expected by the delayed directory cleanup helper.
entries.default: unset (set and overwritten by native Setup)

consumers.description: Delayed cleanup helper refuses deletion when the protected journal belongs to another transaction

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_CLEANUP_WAIT_MS
entries.purpose: Bounded parent-process wait duration for the native Setup delayed directory cleanup helper.
entries.default: unset (set and overwritten by native Setup)

consumers.description: Delayed cleanup helper exits without deletion if the originating Setup process does not stop within this bound

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESSUTIL_INHERITED_OUTPUT_CHILD
entries.purpose: Selects the inherited-output descendant subprocess fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows inherited-output test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESSUTIL_INHERITED_OUTPUT_HELPER
entries.purpose: Selects the inherited-output parent subprocess fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows inherited-output test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESS_TREE_GRANDCHILD
entries.purpose: Selects the process-tree grandchild subprocess fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows process-tree test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESS_TREE_HELPER
entries.purpose: Selects the process-tree parent subprocess fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows process-tree test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESS_TREE_MARKER
entries.purpose: Marker path written by the managed-breakaway process-tree fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows process-tree test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_PROCESS_TREE_PID_FILE
entries.purpose: PID-record path shared with the process-tree cancellation fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows process-tree test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SETUP_CANCEL_TEST_HELPER
entries.purpose: Selects the parent-cancellation native Setup helper subprocess.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_SETUP_TIMEOUT_TEST_HELPER
entries.purpose: Selects the bounded native Setup child-timeout helper.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_LAUNCHER_LOGICAL_CWD
entries.purpose: Carries the expected logical working directory to the long-CWD launcher fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_LAUNCHER_LONG_CWD_HELPER
entries.purpose: Selects the isolated long-CWD launcher helper subprocess.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_GRACEFUL_STOP_MARKER
entries.purpose: Carries the marker used by the authenticated graceful-stop daemon fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_GRACEFUL_STOP_OBSERVED
entries.purpose: Carries the observation marker used to prove the daemon graceful-stop fixture received its shutdown request.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_GRACEFUL_STOP_RELEASE
entries.purpose: Carries the release marker used to let the daemon graceful-stop fixture finish after observing shutdown.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_WATCHDOG_WAIT_READY
entries.purpose: Carries the readiness marker used by the native watchdog original-handle wait fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_WATCHDOG_WAIT_EXIT
entries.purpose: Selects the native watchdog original-handle wait fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_TEST_WINDOWS_NOTIFICATION
entries.purpose: Enables the attended native Windows notification delivery test.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_AUTHENTICODE_HELPER
entries.purpose: Carries the Authenticode PowerShell helper path to the native unsigned-PE regression fixture.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_UNSIGNED_PE
entries.purpose: Carries an unsigned portable-executable fixture path to the native Authenticode evidence regression.

consumers.description: Native Windows test fixture; never read by production runtime paths

entries.name: DEFENSECLAW_GATEWAY_URL
entries.purpose: Base URL of an external gateway for the security-suite e2e tier (TestSecuritySuiteE2E). When set, the test posts to that gateway's /api/v1/inspect/* endpoints; when unset, the tier runs against an in-process inspect server.

consumers.description: TestSecuritySuiteE2E reads this to locate the gateway; uses an in-process server when unset
